programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Double Trouble
programme.desc: Dog's feeling lucky, so he's going to try to capture two women in one day. The first, Moani, has "crazy" tattooed on her neck and is facing drug charges. The second fugitive, Jade, who turns out to be a tough catch, despite help from Jade's sister.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Big Island, Small Town
programme.desc: Dog has problems when he goes to a small town on the Big Island of Hawaii to track down a fugitive. In a small town, everyone knows everyone else's business, making it impossible for Dog to hunt in secret.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Jonah is Missing

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Suga On My Cuffs
programme.desc: Dog and the team go after Kandice, AKA Candy, AKA Suga, a stripper who didn't make her court appearance. They convince one of her friends to lure her to a supermarket parking lot, where Dog has an easy time fetching the fetching young thing. But then

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Mothers and Daughters
programme.desc: Dog and Beth take on the heartbreaking case of Rachel. Rachel's mom bailed her out of jail on condition that she go to AA meetings and stick to a curfew. But Rachel hasn't kept up her end of the bargain, and now her mother wants her back to jail.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Dog Is Smokin'
programme.desc: Dog is trying to cut down on smoking, but it's not easy with his high-stress job. Today, he must track down Walter, a fugitive who believes that the Canadian military has planted a chip in his tooth that allows him to communicate with his van.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: You Snooze, You Lose
programme.desc: Beth designs a luxurious new office for the team and shows it off to Dog, but the bounty hunter is more interested in starting his hunt for Federico, who's been revoked for missing court dates and generally running amuck.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Battle of the Brothers
programme.desc: Willie's suspicious behavior from dyeing his beard to buying a new motorcycle, makes Jase come to the conclusion that Willie is having a mid-life crisis.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Aloha, Robertsons!
programme.desc: After all their hard work, Willie decides to take the entire Robertson clan on a family vacation to Hawaii. But Willie's daily itinerary for the trip gets constantly undermined by the family's desire to participate in their own island activities.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Till Duck Do Us Part
programme.desc: Everyone comes together to plan a surprise wedding for Phil and Kay, setting up a special ceremony on their land, which the whole family attends. Si distracts Phil and Kay by taking them on a "trip down memory lane".

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: So You Think You Can Date?
programme.desc: After discovering that Martin has to miss a Friday night poker game for a date, the guys (as well as their wives) take turns figuring out the best way to coach him for romance. When babysitting for Jep and Jessica turns into a rocky road.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Hot Tub Grime Machine
programme.desc: The guys realize that Godwin has never really asked them for a favor, even though they are all always helping each other out. Godwin asks them to help him get a hot tub, so they set out to help him pick one up.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Aliens Walk Among Us
programme.desc: Stories that suggest aliens are among us, including an Arizona woman who claims to have been fathered by an alien in a top-secret government experiment.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Falling Man, Space Rocks, Missing Feet
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Falling Man, Space Rocks, Missing Feet: A high-rise window washer somehow survives a 50-storey fall and ball-sized ice chunks fall from the sky.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Sea Lions, Voynich Manuscript, Cocaine Mummies
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Sea Lions, Voynich Manuscript, Cocaine Mummies: A look at a mysterious medieval manuscript and Egyptian mummies containing cocaine from Peru.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Sailing Stones, Brown Mountain Lights, Super Human Runner
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Sailing Stones, Brown Mountain Lights, Super Human Runner: A look at Death Valley boulders, mysterious dancing lights and super-marathon runners.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Dolphin Saviours, Grim Reapers, Hysterical Strength
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Dolphin Saviours, Grim Reapers, Hysterical Strength: A look at dolphin rescues, death-smelling pets and a skinny teen with unbelievable strength.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Time Traveller, American Stonehenge, Plant Perception
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Time Traveller, American Stonehenge, Plant Perception: Check out mind-reading plants, pagan human sacrifice and Californian time travellers.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Burma: The Forgotten Front (Spring 1943)
programme.desc: The Japanese meet resistance from British forces as they advance through Burma to take India in 1944.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Battle of Sicily
programme.desc: Fresh from victory in North Africa, the Allies successfully invade the island of Sicily, staging an eventual Italian withdrawal from the war.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Bombing of Ploesti
programme.desc: American bombers target a Romanian oil refinery in a daring 1943 mission.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Monte Cassino (September 3, 1943)
programme.desc: As the allies make their way into Italy, they encounter tremendous resistance at German defensive positions along the Gustav line. One of these locations is an ancient monastery called Monte Cassino.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: The Gilbert Islands (November 1943)
programme.desc: Bloody fighting erupts on a group of tiny Pacific islands in November 1943.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Battle of Anzio
programme.desc: Allied armies encounter German resistance in the Italian fishing town of Anzio.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Kwajalein
programme.desc: Examining the battle at the Kwajelein Atoll in the South Pacific during 1944. Included: operations by the Seabees.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Battle in Palau (Summer 1944)
programme.desc: As the United States prepared for the invasion of the Philippines, scheduled for late 1944, the tiny Palau Islands had to be taken first.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: D-Day (June 6,1944)
programme.desc: D-Day, the 6th of June--one of the most pivotal moments in world history. In a massive armada of thousands of ships, 3,000,000 allied troops land at Three Beaches in Normandy, France.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Retaking France
programme.desc: Six weeks after the D-Day invasion of the Beaches of Normandy, the allied forces are pushing the Germans back through the French countryside. Town by town is won back as Panzer divisions retreat back to the Meuse River.

programme.title: U-Boat Wargamers: The Mastermind
programme.desc: When WW2 breaks out, an elite force of German U-boat commanders attempt to starve Britain into submission. Vera Mathews heads the reformed WRENS. Meanwhile retired naval commander Gilbert Roberts is desperate to return to his career.

programme.title: Hitler's Engineers
programme.desc: In his role as Adolf Hitler's favourite architect, Albert Speer was a central figure in the Fuhrer's inner circle and helped cement his position in power.

programme.title: Hell On Earth WWII
programme.desc: Facing a Soviet Army that grows stronger and pushes German forces back across the lands they occupied, and facing devastation of German cities by Allied bombers, Goebbels issues a call for “Total War”.

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Russian designer Nikolay decided to engineer a solution to trench warfare with an armoured vehicle with wheels that could move over any terrain.

programme.title: Hitler's Secret Bomb: Episode 2
programme.desc: Did Nazi uranium make it to Japan in the final months of WW2? Were the Axis powers closer to a bomb than we think? Drama unfolds as experts shed new light on the hardships, challenges and secrets of a war-torn, pre-nuclear world.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Fish Fight
programme.desc: For the tuna fishermen of Gloucester, Massachusetts, it's tournament time, and, with a big cash prize up for grabs, the fight is on to catch the biggest bluefin.

programme.title: Arthur: The World of Tomorrow/Is There a Doctor in the House?
programme.desc: Since Binky's repeating third grade, he's totally dreading Mr. Ratburn's annual class sleepover at the Science Museum. Arthur and D.W. face the ultimate challenge when Mom gets a bad cold and then gives it to Dad!

programme.title: Arthur: What's Cooking?/Buster's Special Delivery
programme.desc: Celebrity chef Ming Tsai is judging Lakewood Elementary's first-ever cooking contest, and Arthur thinks he has a shot at winning. Then, Buster volunteers to deliver the school mail and finds out that his new job is actually hard work!

programme.title: Arthur: Water and The Brain/Arthur The Unfunny
programme.desc: Is The Brain a snob? That seems to be the explanation for why he won't go to Waterworld with his friends. And, Arthur's a notorious joke-killer. But when he's scheduled to perform as a carnival clown, he learns how to make people laugh!

programme.title: Arthur: D.W.'s Name Game/Finders Key-Pers
programme.desc: D.W. and Arthur get their feelings hurt when their efforts to insult each other get out of control. Next, Arthur, Brain, and Binky find a key--but to what? And which of them will get to keep whatever fabulous thing they find with it?

programme.title: Arthur: Opposites Distract/Just the Ticket
programme.desc: Arthur and Buster have always been best friends, but when a leaky roof forces Arthur to study in Buster's room, they discover that they make better friends than roommates. Arthur's ecstatic when he wins free concert tickets on the radio.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Underwear/Francine Frensky, Olympic Rider
programme.desc: Arthur thinks it's hilarious when Binky Barnes' pants split during class--until he starts having nightmares about it happening to him. Then, Francine secretly dreams of being an Olympic equestrienne, but first she has to learn to ride.

programme.title: Arthur: Paradise Lost/The Pride of Lakewood
programme.desc: Baby Kate is growing up! But strangely enough, the more words she learns, the less she seems to understand their dog. To boost school spirit, Arthur and his classmates form the Lakewood Pride Committee, but not everyone wants to join.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's New Puppy/Arthur Bounces Back
programme.desc: Arthur's new puppy is exactly what he wanted. He's cute and wriggly, and he follows Arthur everywhere. But training a puppy is harder than Arthur thought. Then, a mere $10 stands between Arthur and the shoes he's been dreaming about.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Chicken Pox/Sick As A Dog
programme.desc: The circus is in town and the whole family's excited—until Arthur gets CHICKEN POX! Will he recover in time? Then, Arthur's worst fears come true when poor, sick Pal has to sleep overnight at the vet's office. Could it be something he did?

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: As he watches birds fly overhead, Riff begins to daydream of flying himself. After seeing all the Moms playing with their kids in the Park, Baby Bop wonders what makes somebody a Mommy.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Venice, Anyone?
programme.desc: Emma can't play with her friends because she has to complete a school project about her family. Before you know it, Barney brings Emma on a trip to Venice to learn more about her great granddad.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ and Riff find a pirate map, but in order to find the treasure, they have to be able to count. It’s time for a music concert in the park.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop can't find anybody to play with her. When the others see her feeling sad, they decide to play whatever she wants to play. The dinos and kids are planning a camp-out at the park.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: A creepy spider inspires Barney to tell the story of “The Reluctant Dragon.”

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop is surprised by the first day of cold weather, Barney helps her see that winter is a wonderful time. On a hot summer day, Barney teaches BJ about all the activities you can do in the summer.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Rabbits - Listen
programme.desc: BJ is pretending to be a great detective. But all great detectives need a mystery to solve! One quickly comes his way when an animal nibbles on one of Baby Bop's healthy snacks.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Little Red Rock'n Hood - Differences
programme.desc: Baby Bop is having a hard time finding a story she hasn't heard before. Barney and the little dinos decide to tell her an old story with a new twist! They tell the story of "Little Red Riding Hood" in a whole new way...by singing it!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Michelangelo Meets Mondo Gecko
programme.desc: Michelangelo is turtle-napped by mutant lizard Mondo Gecko, who was mutated at the same time and place as the Turtles.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Enter: Mutagen Man
programme.desc: Krang plans to use a large rocket's vapour trail to rain mutagen over the city when nerdy delivery boy Seymour Guts falls into Shredder's mutagen vat.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Donatello's Badd Time
programme.desc: Hillybilly crooks Bedelia Badd and sons Bufrod and Bubbaheist the Turtles new improved van, using it to steal the diamond in Dr. Kepple's Powercizer, which drains the city's power and could destroy it.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Michelangelo Meets Bugman - Again!
programme.desc: The Swatter threatens to destroy the city with a stolen swarm of super termites unless the Turtles meet him atop The Entire State Building.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Muckman Messes Up!
programme.desc: Krang sends Shredder, Rocksteady and Bebop after new mutagen Compound X-7, which Krang thinks will un-mutate Turtles. At lab, garbagemen are doused with the stuff and change into mutants Muckman and Joe Eyeball, blaming Turtles.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Napoleon Bullfrog: Colossus of the Swamps
programme.desc: Krang uses Big Louie to steal anti-freeze to try to start the Technodrome, now stuck in the Arctic. Shredder zaps Turtle pal Napoleon Bullfrog, with mutagen, who mutates into a giant monster and escapes into the city.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Raphael vs. The Volcano
programme.desc: Raphael, convinced he is dying due to a faulty reading by Donatell's Health-O-Meter, becomes do-gooder THE Green Defender, popping up around the city foiling crime.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Donatello's Duplicate
programme.desc: Donatello creates an egotistical Clone of himself who stuffs Donatello in a trunk.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Ice Creature Cometh
programme.desc: Bebop dumps a vat of mutagen into the Arctic ice, creating a gigantic Ice Monster, who Krang unleashes on the city to help him steal thermal nuclear devices to free the Technodrome.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Leonardo Cuts Loose
programme.desc: Muscleman fiend Wally Airhead captures Turtles with a runaway exercise machine trap to add their powers to his race of super musclemen, but Turtles escape.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is impressed by the TV show of a magician who transforms a partridge into a bouquet of flowers. To get rid of the Lemmings once and for all, for they exasperate him with their incessant games, Grizzy tries the magic trick on them.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's TV remote control is stolen by a partridge who collects high tech objects. This is a case of absolute urgency. Grizzy has no other choice but to requisition the Lemmings' propulsion game to recover his possession.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have discovered a truly wild new game, an augmented reality programme on the ranger's digital tablet. Grizzy confiscates this toy and becomes a fan of the salmon fishing game.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The only edible food item left in the ranger's cabin is a salmon stuck inside a block of ice. Starving, Grizzy decides to melt the ice with the blow dryer. Only the Lemmings are already using it for their latest rocket game in the piping system.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have unearthed a box filled with birthday fountains of light that spit forth giant bursts of sparks. They have a blast, but their new game is not really compatible with a bear's nap.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's last jar of chocolate spread has been pinched by the Lemmings, who are savouring it in secret.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Wedged in his sofa to relieve his aching back, Grizzly baulks at getting up. He designs clever strategies to do everything from a distance, including confiscating the Lemming's radio-controlled toy robot transformer, which he will use as a butler.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The vibrations created by Grizzy's snoring during his nap give the Lemmings an idea for a bouncing game. But when Grizzy changes positions and his snoring stops, the Lemmings must find other sources of vibrations.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a birthday piñata in the ranger's living room. The idea of bursting open a papier-mâché statue with a bat as it says in the instructions pleases him.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Inside a mysterious cave, Grizzy and the Lemmings discover a magical Game of the Goose. The events displayed in the squares that the players land on come true in real life.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is bothered by an object stuck in between the sofa cushions. It's a big fur lemming puppet glove and the Lemmings believe that the puppet is a Super Lemming who's stronger than Grizzy and they start to obey it.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: With his four Orange League badges in hand, Ash has one last challenge to face before he can be crowned Champion of the Orange League. Does he have what it takes?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash’s battle for the Orange League Winner’s Trophy rages on, and it soon becomes apparent that he will have to take on a powerful Dragonite to become the Orange League Champion!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Trying to rescue a local school of Lapras from would-be poachers, our heroes must find a way to convince these distrustful Pokémon they are friends...before it’s too late!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: With Pallet Town on their minds, our heroes are searching for a ferry, but soon discover the port is abandoned...except for some explosive Electrode! Can Ash stop Team Rocket before the ferry goes up in smoke?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash has an eventful homecoming with a surprise visit from an old friend, a visit to see Professor Oak, and a Team Rocket raid. There’s no place like home!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: After Gary comes to the rescue against Team Rocket, Ash seizes an opportunity…at long last, our hero gets to challenge his rival to their very first Pokémon battle!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash begins his journey in Johto, a region largely unexplored and populated with Pokémon entirely new to him and his friends.

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: On the road again, Ash and his Charizard soundly defeat an ambitious rookie Trainer named Casey. Upset, she runs away before Ash can comfort her—the perfect time for Team Rocket to strike!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: In a forest that's being destroyed by rampaging Pinsir, Ash has to enlist the help of the local Heracross to protect their home! But it turns out these Pokémon look tougher than they act…

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys

A family man struggling to hold it all together discovers a hidden parlor that offers a solution...sexbots.

programme.title: Time Runners

programme.title: The Silver Brumby

programme.desc: Dog pursues two people whose lives have been ruined by drugs. First, he tracks Mae, who traded a good home and loving family for an ice pipe. The next case involves Jude. His addiction has caused his pregnant wife to fear for her safety.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Do Unto Others
programme.desc: Dog is a rough, tough bounty hunter, but he's a softie at heart, as the two cases in this episode show.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: A Helping Hand
programme.desc: Dog answers two very different calls for help from women. In the first case, he helps Leland protect Maria, who's being threatened by her violent husband. Then Dog visit a young woman who's dying and has asked to see Dog as one of her final wishes.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Take Your Daughter to Work Day
programme.desc: Baby Lyssa, who's just turned 18 and is studying to become a bondsman, gets to do some on-the-job training when she helps Dog hunt for Wayne, who's run out on a bond for second-degree assault.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: In Sickness and In Health
programme.desc: Dog is sick as a dog, but he still must lead the hunt for a fugitive named James. James' girlfriend tries to cover for him, but Dog surprises James in his bedroom and leads him away in cuffs.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: If the Shirt Fits...
programme.desc: Beth buys Dog a shirt with an embroidered chicken, but Dog explains he only wears shirts with pictures of either Jesus or a Harley-Davidson. When it's time to go to work, the team goes after Necia, a fugitive with felony charges pending.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: The Thief Who Stole Christmas
programme.desc: It's Christmas day, and Dog sets out to nab Raymond, who robs to support his ice habit, and give him a little Christmas gift - shiny new handcuffs.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Rock-a-Bye Bounty
programme.desc: Dog and Beth must go after their former housekeeper, Lisa, who's jumped bond. They know this case will be tricky because Lisa knows everyone in the family. Luckily, Beth's nephew, Jason, is visiting and is quickly put to work as the bait.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Hot Tub Grime Machine
programme.desc: The guys realize that Godwin has never really asked them for a favor, even though they are all always helping each other out. Godwin asks them to help him get a hot tub, so they set out to help him pick one up.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: A-Jase-ent Living
programme.desc: Due to construction at Jase and Missy's house, Willie and Korie invite Jase and the kids to live with them while Missy is out of town visiting friends. Then, what begins in the duck call room as a series of "Gentlemen's Bets".

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Termite Be a Problem
programme.desc: Because of a termite problem at Duck Commander, the guys are forced to find a different location to work from - Godwin's House. But while at Godwin's house, Godwin gets a taxidermied gift from a past relative.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: John Luke After Dentist
programme.desc: John Luke and Sadie are getting some dental work done, complete with sedation, and it's up to Willie and Korie to take care of them through the process - including getting them back home safely.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Scoot Along Si
programme.desc: Si shows up to work on a mobility scooter, claiming to have an invisible injury from a fender bender with Willie. After losing her beloved turtle "Mr. T", Ms. Kay enlists Phil on a mission to find a new pet turtle.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Jerky Boys
programme.desc: A decision to try and make some duck jerky leads the boys to Godwin's garage and a minibike race. Phil and Kay have made Willie the executor of their will, and Phil wants to show Willie what he and his brothers will inherit when he and Kay pass away.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Going Si-ral
programme.desc: Si discovers Youtube, and, after watching a series of instructional videos, decides he can make something better. John Luke totals Willie's truck, and Willie realizes he may be raising the two worst drivers in all of Louisiana.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Quack-O-Lantern
programme.desc: The Robertsons host a Halloween event at Duck Commander, where they all come together to transform the warehouse into a "scarehouse." Everyone dresses up and Willie desperately tries to be the scariest, but none of the kids are afraid of him.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: O Little Town of West Monroe
programme.desc: The Robertsons perform a live nativity for their Church for Christmas, so the wives organize rehearsals to ensure that they pull off a great event. However, rehearsals get off to a bumpy start as the guys can't stop sharing their opinions.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Boomerang Becca
programme.desc: When Rebecca returns home from her internship in Los Angeles, the Robertson family plans a big surprise party to welcome her back. Meanwhile, when Uncle Si gets sick, he seeks the help of Ms. Kay to take care of him.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Time Traveller, American Stonehenge, Plant Perception
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Time Traveller, American Stonehenge, Plant Perception: Check out mind-reading plants, pagan human sacrifice and Californian time travellers.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Mysterious Vanishings
programme.desc: Mysterious Vanishings: Baffling stories of people who vanished without a trace, including a young Tasmanian pilot who is chased by unidentified aircraft before disappearing.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Bullet Proof Boobs, Amazing Coincidences, Directional Cows
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Bullet Proof Boobs, Amazing Coincidences, Directional Cows: Cows align with the Earth's magnetic field, and a young makes an incredible connection.

programme.title: Weird or What? With William Shatner: End of the World
programme.desc: Whether a super-volcano at Yellowstone National Park, Wyoming, could destroy civilization, and the likelihood of killer solar storms.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Lightning Strikes, Human Hibernation, Chinese Anchors
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Lightning Strikes, Human Hibernation, Chinese Anchors: A man survives six lightning strikes, while a toddler is frozen solid and thawed unharmed.

programme.title: Weird or What? With William Shatner: Monsters
programme.desc: A mysterious creature washed up on a Newfoundland beach, an animal attacking with its claws in New Delhi, and a winged beast haunting Virginia.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Retaking France
programme.desc: Six weeks after the D-Day invasion of the Beaches of Normandy, the allied forces are pushing the Germans back through the French countryside. Town by town is won back as Panzer divisions retreat back to the Meuse River.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Operation Dragoon (August 1944)
programme.desc: The Allies break into France and push the Germans back to their own border.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Liberation of the Paris
programme.desc: Both political and military expediency were factors in the return of the French capital to its people.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Arnhem
programme.desc: The Allies launch an attack designed to push the Germans back to the Rhine River.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory
programme.desc: Gen. Douglas MacArthur's return to the Philippines, and the final push to retake the islands after two years of Japanese occupation.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: The Last Stronghold (November 1944)
programme.desc: American B-29 high-altitude bomber attacks Japan near the end of World War II.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Mount Belvedere
programme.desc: The Battle of the Gothic Line in 1944.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Battle of the Bulge (December 1944)
programme.desc: The December 1944 battle pitting American forces against German Panzer divisions in the Ardennes Forest.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Mount Hot Rocks (February 19, 1945)
programme.desc: American forces landing on the shores of Iwo Jima encounter Japanese combatants willing to fight to the death.

programme.title: Hitler's Secret Bomb: Episode 2
programme.desc: Did Nazi uranium make it to Japan in the final months of WW2? Were the Axis powers closer to a bomb than we think? Drama unfolds as experts shed new light on the hardships, challenges and secrets of a war-torn, pre-nuclear world.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Destroyers and Cruisers showcases ships that with their speed and firepower became the backbones of 20th century navies. We tell the incredible story of USS Laffey, the WW2 destroyer that would come to be known as ‘the ship that would not die’.

programme.title: D Day: 100 Days To Beat The Third Reich
programme.desc: This documentary will bring back to life the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. 100 critical days, both heroic and tragic, that made history.

programme.title: History's Greatest Aircraft: HE-111: Germany’s Frontline Bomber
programme.desc: The Heinkel He 111 is one of the most recognised German aircraft of WW2. The broad winged design was a guinea pig for future bombers, with more variants than any other military aircraft of the time.

programme.title: Combat Trains
programme.desc: Four railway journeys that changed modern history: 24-year-old Winston Churchill boarded an armoured train during the Boer War hoping for a newspaper scoop.

programme.title: History Of Weapons: Weapons For All
programme.desc: From the crossbow to the Kalashnikov to 3D-printed firearms, weaponry designed for everyone can offer a decisive advantage in warfare.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Meltdown

programme.desc: A truck driver is trapped in his crushed cab after a motorway smash, while in Hull, firefighters handcuff a man to the rails of the Humber Bridge to stop him from jumping.

programme.desc: A woman in Astoria, Ore., battles an angry neighbor who, she claims, takes offense at a bumper sticker on her car; in Plano, Texas, the losing coach of a kids' baseball game gets into a brawl with a parent from the opposing team.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: In Romana, Calif., a glossy car photoshoot is disrupted by the protests of a driver in an SUV who claims the crew is shooting on his driveway; a homeowner returns from work to find his house trashed and a neighbor asleep in the bedroom.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: An ill-tempered man in Tennessee claims his neighbor's new landscaping is causing flooding in his backyard; in California, a neighbor is alarmed when two strangers come looking for a stolen e-bike.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: A very public breakup plays out in front of all the neighbors at a Florida apartment complex; neighbors in upstate New York bicker over temporary snow fencing; a mail courier in Houston stands his ground in a street fight.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: A Utah woman and her neighbor argue over the building's heating; a Brooklyn landlord's boyfriend breaks through a tenant's door with an ax; neighbors in rural Nebraska squabble over the property line dividing their homes.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: A child drops a candy wrapper outside a home in Tennessee, a neighbor's reaction leads to heated words; hundreds of teenagers party in an Arizona garage; in Alabama, a woman is upset by trash outside her apartment complex.

programme.title: Customer Wars
programme.desc: A customer calls the police over a dryer dispute; a fast-food customer demanding a refund slaps an employee; an alligator wrestling show takes a terrifying turn; a dispute erupts when a man believes a woman is wrongfully parked in a handicap spot.

programme.title: Customer Wars
programme.desc: Two passengers deal with flight delays in very different ways; in Groves, Texas, a carnival worker dangles from a ride 30 feet in the air while trying to keep a young rider safe; a food delivery worker in Ohio gives a customer a piece of his mind.

programme.title: Customer Wars
programme.desc: In Seattle, a restaurant owner confronts a woman accused of trying to leave without paying her tab; a rideshare driver in Washington D.C. remains very cool under extreme pressure; in Hawaii, a self-proclaimed Shopping Cart Narc runs for cover.

programme.desc: It's Suzi's birthday and McGee ate her gift!

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: Jet’s skateboard prowess lands him an endorsement deal with a local extreme sports company. The terms of the deal with “Gnarly Sports” requires him to wear the Gnarly Logo on everything, including his Posse gear and weapons.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: The search for the Moonlands Diamond leads the Final Dreamers back to the void energy island. This time, they find themselves in the path of a greedy, treasure-hunting bunch of Realm Raider pirates, who trap Gorgram and steal the gem!

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: Skor needs a brain to complete his latest creation, Frankendino. But the only brain that’s good enough is Edgar’s, so Skor hands Skrap a serrated brain spoon and sends him off to fetch it.

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: The Street Sharks stumble across mysterious Seaviate creatures who live in a vast cavern under the city.

programme.title: The Wombles

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: VENOM has built a fake amusement park to mask the largest solar collector ever built.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Unpacking from a holiday in Mexico, Penfold discovers El Loco hiding in his suitcase. El Loco gets loose and goes on a crime spree in London.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Chicken Pox/Sick As A Dog
programme.desc: The circus is in town and the whole family's excited—until Arthur gets CHICKEN POX! Will he recover in time? Then, Arthur's worst fears come true when poor, sick Pal has to sleep overnight at the vet's office. Could it be something he did?

programme.title: Arthur: The Chips Are Down/Revenge Of The Chip
programme.desc: What a mysterious, beautiful thing a green potato chip is--and D.W. just ate a gigantic one! But then she hears a rumor that they're poisonous...Sure, kids do some pretty embarrassing things, but do Moms have to tell everyone?

programme.title: Arthur: Prunella's Tent of Portent/Mutiny on the Pitch
programme.desc: At the Elwood City Picnic, Prunella pretends she can see the future at her fortune telling booth. The Lakewood Elementary soccer team has had enough of Francine's bossy behavior.

programme.title: Arthur: Vomitrocious!/Sue Ellen Chickens Out
programme.desc: It's just a regular day until Francine starts to feel queasy, and then throws up in the cafeteria! Now Francine is afraid to go back to school. And, Sue Ellen learns that a fast-food chain is taking over the beloved Sugar Bowl.

programme.title: Arthur: D.W.'S Stray Netkitin/Bats in the Belfry
programme.desc: D.W. is panicked when a virus ruins the Read family computer. Who will take care of her virtual pet? Arthur and D.W. are thrilled when Grandma Thora lets them turn her attic into a clubhouse. But will a bat threaten their new play space?

programme.title: Arthur: Fernfern and the Secret of Moose Mountain/Thanks a Lot, Binky
programme.desc: Fern can't wait to climb Moose Mountain so that she can have adventures like Zutzut, the famous comic-book hero! Then, Binky decides to become "No More Mr. Nice Guy" after not getting thanked for saving Rattles from a dangerous stunt.

programme.title: Arthur: Buster's Second Chance/Arthur and the Whole Truth
programme.desc: With the help of a little time travel, Buster turns back the clock to see what his life would be like if he were a genius. Arthur isn't happy when he discovers that Buster has been telling him some white lies, just to spare his feelings.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur vs. The Piano/The Big Blow-Up
programme.desc: Arthur gets stage fright during a rehearsal. Will he be able to get through his recital without any mistakes? Then, two opinionated friends butt heads! Can Francine and The Brain put aside their differences for the good of the soccer team?

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Little Red Rock'n Hood - Differences
programme.desc: Baby Bop is having a hard time finding a story she hasn't heard before. Barney and the little dinos decide to tell her an old story with a new twist! They tell the story of "Little Red Riding Hood" in a whole new way...by singing it!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney and his friends are excited about a fishing contest. Barney tells Riff about a famous musician named Beethoven who wrote beautiful music even when he couldn't hear.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop, BJ and the children prepare for the Shapes Carnival in the park./When it starts to rain at the park, Baby Bop joins Barney and BJ inside the caboose.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop is disappointed when their trip to the zoo is postponed. Barney tells Baby Bop a tale about keeping your promise.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Let's Make Music
programme.desc: Barney, Baby Bop and BJ have fun learning about music, rhythm and musical instruments. Barney introduces many different instruments and BJ dreams of one day becoming a rock 'n roll star. They discover that music gives everyone a happy feelin.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Come Blow Your Horn!
programme.desc: Barney and the kids are excited because five of Mr. Boyd's brass-playing buddies are putting on a show in the park tonight! Angela is learning to play the trumpet and knows that it takes a lot of practice. BJ arrives and the group visits the caboose.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: A Wonderful World of Colors and Shapes
programme.desc: Picture this! When Barney shows off his new painting, his friends are inspired to create their own art show. Discovering how to create new colors and draw shapes, Barney and his friends put on a super-dee-duper art show

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney reminds Riff and BJ that Christmas is about more than getting gifts. Barney takes Melanie on a trip to the North Pole to deliver her letter in person.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop forgets to say "please" and "thank you". Riff shares his dream of becoming an inventor.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Leonardo Cuts Loose
programme.desc: Muscleman fiend Wally Airhead captures Turtles with a runaway exercise machine trap to add their powers to his race of super musclemen, but Turtles escape.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Pirate Radio
programme.desc: Krang plots to take city by luring citizens into an inter­dimensional "Krang's Chasm" at sea by inserting Shredder in place of DJ. Dickie Bark as a Pied Piper on steamship radio station.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Raphael, the Turtle of a Thousand Faces
programme.desc: Raphael disguises himself as Mad Dog McNutt, who is planning a heist of the Natural History museum.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Leonardo, the Renaissance Turtle
programme.desc: Professor Mindbender fools the Mayor into installing Lex, a supercop robot, to clean up crime, but rewires him to arrest practically the whole city.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Zach and the Alien Invaders
programme.desc: Zach's over-active imagination has him seeing Aliens everywhere. Sent to Military School, he discovers actual Aliens posing as officers.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Welcome Back, Polarisoids!
programme.desc: The Polarisoids return to Earth unwittingly invited by Krang, bringing to life the alien Creatures caught as images on tape as a distraction so Krang can break into the city's computer systems.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Planet of the Turtleloids: Part 1
programme.desc: Krang and Shredder transport to the City Wild Animal Park with Bebop and Rocksteady to create an army of Mutants from the animals there.

programme.title: House Hazards

programme.title: House Hazards

programme.title: Hoarders

programme.title: Hoarders
programme.desc: A woman blames her hoarding on demons possessing her home. A man faces charges when the hoard in his historic San Francisco Victorian house catches fire and almost burns down the block.

programme.title: Hoarders
programme.desc: Shanna owns the worst hoard in 'Hoarders' history, a house crammed floor to ceiling with bottles of human waste. Meanwhile, Lynda is hoarding stuff for the Armageddon.

programme.title: Hoarders

programme.title: Hoarders

programme.desc: Jim is a bondsman and longtime friend of Dog. Jim's son has just started in the family business. Today, Jim has asked Dog to track down a potentially dangerous fugitive, and take his son along for some training with the best in the world.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Fishful Thinking

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: The Searchers

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Episode 7
programme.desc: With Danny away, and Du Plessis undertaking an ignominious stretch of community service, Ed is left in charge of running the Endangered Species Project.

programme.desc: As he celebrates his first anniversary, Norman (Matt Frewer) thinks he's the luckiest man alive. But suddenly, his lovely wife doesn't seem the same and he can't stand to be around her. Is he going crazy or are his instincts telling him something?

programme.desc: Problem child Rusty (Ryan Phillippe) finds himself thrown into a private school bent on straightening him out with some unorthodox methods. Rusty has eluded their control so far, but can he escape from this prison of the mind?

programme.desc: Colonel Hogan and Commandant Klink go to London to steal a plane as part of a plan to ferret out German spies in England.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Posing as German officers, the Heroes try to get a German spy to disclose with whom he was working.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Disguised as a German officer's wife, Newkirk collects penicillin for the underground.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: To capture some secret papers, Hogan has a Russian woman convince Klink to volunteer for the Russian front.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan helps prove the German officer that Klink is defending is innocent of treason so he can get the plans that he had stolen for the Allies.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: These DNA Results Will Floor You

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: 13-Year-Old Father?
programme.desc: Curtis sat down with his mother Telisha, and told her that he is trying to have a baby but Telisha was shocked and terrified because Curtis is only 13 years old! Curtis insists he is ready to be a father.

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: Bullied Out of School
programme.desc: Aabrea and Jerae bullied a high school girl so badly that she dropped out. Can Steve help them resolve their differences and figure out why so many teens turn to bullying?

programme.desc: Gina believes that her daughter’s boyfriend Efrain is abusing her grandson. Her daughter wants to prove that Efrain is innocent, but her father Raul suspects she is covering for Efrain.

programme.desc: Megan is 14 and pregnant with 19 year old Michael’s baby, but he doesn’t see any problems with this situation. Laurie, Megan’s mother, turns to Steve to help get through to her!

programme.title: The Adventures Of Sherlock Holmes

programme.title: The Adventures Of Sherlock Holmes

programme.title: The Adventures Of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An abandoned baby is kidnapped and the detectives must find the baby and his father.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: The Case of the Royal Murder

programme.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles

programme.title: Sherlock: A Case of Evil
programme.desc: This tale focuses on the young 20-something Sherlock Holmes. Sherlock is in pursuit of the evil Moriarty and apparently shoots him to death, but his body cannot be found as he falls in a sewer.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Each team must present chef Ramsay with a dish made with king crab; each team gets to create their own menus for the dinner service.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Each chef must use 14 ingredients to create a dish to impress chef Ramsay; one chef is sent to the medic.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The contestants visit the Borgata Hotel Casino & Spa, where one of them will receive a position as head chef; each contestant must create a signature dish.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Chef Ramsay prepares dishes for the contestants to taste and re-create; a proposal is planned during the dinner service.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The final three chefs must prepare a meal for 100 people, including chefs from Los Angeles' finest restaurants.

programme.title: Run's House: Growing Up Is Hard To Do
programme.desc: Believing Diggy's fly wardrobe may have something to do with girls being interested in him, Russy raids Diggy's closet. Hearing the boys argue over clothes, Justine decides to get Russy some clothes of his own.

programme.title: Run's House: Run's Manner House
programme.desc: Rev and Justine are both fed up with the boys' rude behavior. Justine decides to bring in a supernanny type person to teach the family the basics in social etiquette.

programme.title: Run's House: When Diggy Met Brittney
programme.desc: When Rev learns that Diggy's play date is actually with two girls he believes that Diggy must have a crush on one of them. He's hoping it's Ja Rule's daughter because they must be rich.

programme.title: Run's House: Rapper's Retreat
programme.desc: Rev receives guest passes to the Mohegan Sun in Connecticut. Justine doesn't want to go. Rev is proud of Jojo's recent recordings with Blackout and wants to reward him as a fellow rapper.

programme.title: Run's House: Camp Rev

programme.title: Run's House: Who's Your Daddy?
programme.desc: While putting together her latest insert of Angela's Rundown, Angela discovers she has way too much content to fit into one sixteen-page insert. She and her crew at work discuss a full magazine.

programme.title: Run's House: Rock in Run's House

programme.title: Run's House: Baby Steps

programme.title: Run's House: Mixed and Mastered

programme.title: Half & Half: The Big Diva Down Episode

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Basement

programme.desc: PRS investigates the Campbell House, where the Innkeeper and guests claim to have sexual encounters with a spirit who has deep ties to a family tragedy.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Shadow Caster

programme.title: Paranormal State: Satan's Soldier

programme.desc: Brenda is terrified by a dark hooded figure spotted in her West Virginia home. The same entity allegedly tormented her late husband. PRS and Psychic/Medium Michelle Belanger help Brenda before she slips into the same abyss as her late husband.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Todmoredon Church

programme.title: Most Haunted: Weir Mill
programme.desc: Yvette take her team to an abandoned mill where spirits are said to roam and satanic ritual taken place. What awaits them is an evening of fear that causes one of the team to leave early.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Ripon Workhouse

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Allen's Ruff Day

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Could vampires be real? A well-known U.S. paranormal investigator and folklorist in New York City recounts the legend and evolution of the vampire throughout history and from culture to culture.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: An investigation into the various conspiracy theories revolving around the tragic death of Diana, The People's Princess, in Paris in August 1997.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Were the Mercury, Genesis, Apollo and Space Shuttle programs mere window dressing designed for public consumption?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: The legend of the werewolf throughout history and in various cultures is explored. The Conspiracy Show travels to England and rural Wisconsin to investigate claims of actual werewolf sightings.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Rise of the Fourth Reich (Nazi's in America)
programme.desc: The Allies defeated the Axis Powers on the battlefield, but did they actually win the war? Richard interviews several alternative historians who say that while the German Army may have surrendered in 1945, the Nazi's never officially did.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: A serious investigation of the long-held theory that Elvis Presley faked his death in August 1977. Jokes of Elvis sightings in Kalamazoo aside, there is a considerable volume of evidence to suggest Elvis may not have left the building

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1995 Paris, Guy Georges, a serial killer, was linked to multiple murders through DNA and later confessed in court. He was sentenced to life in prison, leading to the establishment of a DNA database for offenders in France.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2006, Nina Sharanova vanished in Oakland. Her ex-husband, Hans Reiser, was convicted of her murder after their son’s testimony and leading police to her body. Reiser is now appealing the conviction.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1990, Danny Rolling killed five students in Gainesville, Florida. He confessed to the crimes, pleaded guilty to avoid the death penalty, but was ultimately sentenced to death and executed 16 years later.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: This is the tragic tale of Susan Smith, who shocked the nation in 1994 by fabricating a carjacking to cover up the drowning murders of her two young sons.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In a gruesome 1989 case, Tracey Wigginton and her accomplices lure Edward Baldock with promises of a good time, only to brutally stab him to death, sparking a sensational trial that raised questions about the roles of her accomplices.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: University students Holly Dunn and Chris Maier are ambushed along railway tracks in August 1997. By 1999, a string of similar attacks leads police to Angel Maturino Reséndiz, who travels by freight train between murders across three states.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2004, Marc Dutroux was sentenced to life for kidnapping, raping, and murdering young girls in Belgium. His 1996 arrest and delayed trial exposed horrific crimes and official failings, sparking public outrage.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1983, Graeme Cunningham vanishes in Salt Lake City. Detective Don Bell discovers Arthur Bishop, who confesses to killing Graeme and four other boys. Bishop is found guilty in 1984 and executed in 1988.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: MEGAFLOODS
programme.desc: Water covers two thirds of the earth’s surface, and we are utterly dependent on it, but when this vital ingredient of life unleashes its fury on the planet it becomes the ultimate bringer of death.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: CONSTRUCTION
programme.desc: Bridges and tunnels have transformed the way we live, making the inaccessible easy to reach, and turning time consuming journeys into convenient commutes, but when things go wrong they can go very wrong indeed.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: HURRICANES
programme.desc: Hurricanes, Typhoons and Cyclones all the same thing with different names – what they also have in common is their destructive power! Hurricanes are large, swirling storms and winds from a hurricane can damage buildings or trees.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: EARTHQUAKES
programme.desc: Earthquakes happen when tectonic plates suddenly slip causing shock waves to shake the surface of the Earth. They occur on fault lines - the most active are the Ring of Fire in the Pacific Ocean and the San Andreas fault on America’s west coast.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: VOLCANOES
programme.desc: Volcanoes are Earth’s geologic architects. They’ve created more than 80 percent of our planet’s surface and their explosive force has crafted both mountains and craters.

programme.title: In the Shadow of the Moon
programme.desc: Between 1968 and 1972, the world watched in awe each time an American spacecraft voyaged to the Moon. Only 12 men walked upon its surface and they remain the only human beings to have stood on another world.

programme.title: Inside Outer Space: Episode 8: Extravehicular Activity
programme.desc: Come with us as we explore and unravel the mysteries of what lies beyond our planet Earth. This is a series packed with space stories and information about our universe covering what we can see.

programme.title: Inside Outer Space: Episode 2: Moons
programme.desc: Come with us as we explore and unravel the mysteries of what lies beyond our planet Earth. This is a series packed with space stories and information about our universe covering what we can see.

programme.title: Inside Outer Space: Episode 1: The Solar System
programme.desc: This is a series packed with space stories and information about our universe covering what we can see, how we can live in space and what science tells us about the past and the future as we journey inside out of space.

programme.title: Inside Outer Space: Episode 4: Black Holes
programme.desc: Come with us as we explore and unravel the mysteries of what lies beyond our planet Earth. This is a series packed with space stories and information about our universe covering what we can see.

programme.title: Inside Outer Space: Episode 7: The Milky Way
programme.desc: Come with us as we explore and unravel the mysteries of what lies beyond our planet Earth. This is a series packed with space stories and information about our universe covering what we can see.

programme.title: Jungle Planet
programme.desc: The south of Africa is home to an unusual forest that loses its leaves during the dry season. The Mopane tree - or butterfly tree - is the main exponent of this behaviour.

programme.title: Australia's Wild Places: The Coast of the Whalesharks
programme.desc: On the remote coastline of Australia’s northwest, there are great natural wonders to be found. Above the sea on this sunbaked coast, there is very little; only a sparse landscape shaped by the arid conditions. But here, the miracle reveals itself…

programme.title: Wildest Latin America: Amazon: One Jungle, Many Worlds
programme.desc: The Amazon is the largest rainforest in the world, and the richest ecosystem on earth. This film uncovers what makes the Amazon such a powerhouse of evolution; how it has come to home a third of all species on the planet.

programme.title: Wildest Arctic: Taiga: The Frozen Forest
programme.desc: Containing one third of the world's trees, the frozen forests of the Taiga are the lungs of the planet. This great forest spans across shores of the Pacific, sheltering some of the Earth's most majestic animals, and some of its most secretive.

programme.title: Wild Assassins: Tusk and Horns
programme.desc: Welcome to the world of wild assassins, where creatures with weapons carved from bone and ivory stalk the open plains.

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges: The Tyne Bridge
programme.desc: Rob Bell explores the story of the Tyne Bridge.

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges: Tower Bridge
programme.desc: Rob Bell uncovers the extraordinary story of Tower Bridge.

programme.desc: Rob Bell recounts the story of the Britannia Bridge.

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges: The Humber Bridge

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Israel, the West Bank and Gaza are three locations Tony has never travelled to before.The intense and fraught relationship between these places is hard to understand but Tony will attempt to delve into the lives of those who live here, through their food.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: After eight years, Tony returns to South Korea. And this time, he’s no stranger to kimchi. But just as Tony has changed, so has Korea.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Tony explores the other Hawaii, the one that those eight million tourists don’t see when they come to the islands. He discovers the fabric of a true cultural mixing pot.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: In this episode of Parts Unknown, Tony returns to one of his favourite places on Earth, Vietnam.He journeys to a city he has never been to, Hu?, The City of Ghosts.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Rock-a-Bye Bounty
programme.desc: Dog and Beth must go after their former housekeeper, Lisa, who's jumped bond. They know this case will be tricky because Lisa knows everyone in the family. Luckily, Beth's nephew, Jason, is visiting and is quickly put to work as the bait.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Father-Hood
programme.desc: Before Tim heads out for a tough day of bounty hunting, he likes to get in a little quality time with his daughter, Summer Rain.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Cops and Criminals
programme.desc: After giving a rousing speech to an audience of enthusiastic grade-schoolers, Dog catches up with an old friend, Sheriff Tommy Cayetano, to talk business. Dog's got a tough fugitive, named Mia, to catch and Tommy is happy to lend a hand.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Cupid in Cuffs
programme.desc: It's the day before Valentine's Day and love is in the air at Da Kine Bail Bonds--but not for Tapi who has failed to appear in court on theft charges. Dog's plan is to have Leland knock on Tapi's door with a Valentine's gift to draw her out.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Bonds of Love
programme.desc: Dog and Beth are finally getting married! They've been together for more than ten years but Dog is determined, as he puts it, to "marry his common law wife the Christian way."

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: To Love and to Cherish
programme.desc: Dog and Beth are off to the Big Island for their wedding. They hope to relax for a few days but it turns out Leland has a fugitive he needs to catch and despite the time limit, neither Dog nor Beth can resist the opportunity to catch a fugitive.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Bustin' with Justin
programme.desc: Dog and the team must deal with misleading informants, belligerent boaters, and angry drunks when they go after Kimberly, a traffic offender with over 20 violations. And if that isn't enough, Dog has another problem - Duane Lee is sick.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Run Fugitive, Run
programme.desc: When the team hunts for Steven, a fugitive with a history of domestic abuse, they seek help from his girlfriend, who's been a victim of his abuse. Meanwhile, Beth trains for a 5K race to help raise awareness about domestic abuse.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Vegas or Bust
programme.desc: Dog is going to be the keynote speaker at the annual Profession Bail Agents convention in Las Vegas, but before he leaves he's got to clear two cases from his docket - Heidi and Stephanie.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Boomerang Becca
programme.desc: When Rebecca returns home from her internship in Los Angeles, the Robertson family plans a big surprise party to welcome her back. Meanwhile, when Uncle Si gets sick, he seeks the help of Ms. Kay to take care of him.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Willie's Number Two
programme.desc: When Willie is looking to hire a new assistant, Korie insists he interviews her cousin, JD. Meanwhile, when Uncle Si spends the day over at Phil and Kay's, Jep stops by with the kids to keep them away from Jessica, who has taken ill.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Life of Si
programme.desc: After seeing footage Jep shot using a GoPro, Si decides he wants to use the camera to record a day in his life. But when Si gets on the guys nerves while they're hunting, they kick him and his GoPros out of the blind.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: From Duck 'til Dawn
programme.desc: When the duck guys have to pull an all-nighter to finish some work, Willie decides he wants to join them. But when a dying smoke detector distracts them from their work, Jase and Willie head off to disarm it.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Burger Commander
programme.desc: After visiting a popular burger restaurant, Willie and Jase get into a debate over which of them cooks the best burger, prompting them to challenge one another to a cooking contest.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: G.I. Si
programme.desc: When Willie and Jase argue over an old G.I. Joe toy, it leads them into a debate over who would make a better soldier. To settle the score, Willie and Jase head out to the woods with their friends and children for a paintball match.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Jase and the Argonauts
programme.desc: When Willie gets a soft serve machine for himself, he tells the guys they can't use it until they're done working. But when Jase thinks this is unfair, he takes matters into his own hands and finds a better object to use as motivation.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Fowl Playhouse
programme.desc: When Jase finds out Jep is planning to buy his kids a fancy playhouse from a catalogue, he and the guys decide to build one for Jep themselves. But the playhouse ends up looking more like a military-style duck blind.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: The Big LeCOWski
programme.desc: When Sadie spends the day at Duck Commander for a school project, the guys try to show her the ins-and-outs of how a successful company works. But when Willie questions Jase's ingenuity in creating new products they get into a debate.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: The Robertson family pulls together a family reunion to give Mia a big send-off before her surgery.

programme.title: Weird or What? With William Shatner: Monsters
programme.desc: A mysterious creature washed up on a Newfoundland beach, an animal attacking with its claws in New Delhi, and a winged beast haunting Virginia.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Gateway to Hell: A fire-breathing crater burns in the middle of a desert, a deadly shark lurks on a golf course, and a giant white blob threatens a town in California.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Wrath of Thor: A mysterious flame appears in the Norwegian wilderness and video footage shows a ghostly figure stalking people in Japan, confirming a local legend.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Doomsday at Yellowstone: Scientists seek to explain the unexplainable while investigating blue fire near Yellowstone, turkeys circling a dead cat and a Gettysberg ghost.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: The Beast That Ate Jaws: A shark is eaten by a mysterious predator, and experts wonder if a prehistoric monster is to blame. A sinister sight is filmed near Russia's Area 51.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Mount Hot Rocks (February 19, 1945)
programme.desc: American forces landing on the shores of Iwo Jima encounter Japanese combatants willing to fight to the death.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Battle of Remagen
programme.desc: German forces retreat across the Rhine on the Remagen bridge near the close of the war.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Battle of Okinawa
programme.desc: Kamikaze pilots guard the island of Okinawa from American forces.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Fall of Berlin
programme.desc: In 1945, Soviet and American troops press Hitler's armies from the west and the east, culminating in the fall of Berlin.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Commerce Raiders
programme.desc: Heavily armed vessels disguised as ordinary cargo ships patrolled allied waters, sinking supply boats and stealing their content.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Bombing of Germany
programme.desc: The development of the B-17 bomber, which allowed American planes to reach inside Germany and bomb its cities.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: U-Boat War
programme.desc: U-boats posed the deadliest threat to Allied convoys traveling the Atlantic.

programme.title: Allied Commanders of WWII
programme.desc: Heroism was not unique to US commanders, these generals battled the Nazis on all fronts. A look at Bernard Law Montgomery, Georgi Zhukov, and Sir Arthur Harris.

programme.title: Allied Commanders of WWII

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur vs. The Piano/The Big Blow-Up
programme.desc: Arthur gets stage fright during a rehearsal. Will he be able to get through his recital without any mistakes? Then, two opinionated friends butt heads! Can Francine and The Brain put aside their differences for the good of the soccer team?

programme.title: Arthur: Home Sweet Home/Do You Believe in Magic
programme.desc: It’s time to celebrate summer! Join Arthur as he takes a family vacation and visits Grandpa Dave’s farm. Then, Arthur, D.W. and Buster spend a few days at the beach where they discover the simple joys of building sandcastles!

programme.title: Arthur: Binky Goes Nuts/Breezy Listening Blues
programme.desc: When Binky discovers that he's allergic to peanuts, he has so many questions, but learns that the best way not to go nuts is to be well informed and to understand his allergy. Much to his horror, Brain gets a "B-" on a test.

programme.title: Arthur: Is That Kosher/Never Never Never
programme.desc: Francine's convinced that she can fast on Yom Kippur, but what about Arthur’s pizza party? D.W learns that giving gifts isn't the only way of showing you care. Featuring the voice of Joan Rivers.

programme.title: Arthur: The Good Sport/Crushed
programme.desc: When the Athlete of the Year Award doesn't go to Francine, she makes no effort to hide her hurt feelings from the recipient. Arthur can't believe his parents are hiring a babysitter--he's 8 years old, and babysitters are b-o-r-i-n-g!

programme.title: Arthur: Buster's Garden of Grief/Through the Looking Glasses
programme.desc: Buster is in charge of the community garden, but it proves to be too much for just one person. Arthur can't find his glasses and has to get new ones, but the attention he is getting from these new glasses has gone straight to his head!

programme.title: Arthur: Speak Up Francine/Waiting for Snow
programme.desc: The local creek is polluted and pesticide run-off is to blame. Francine is determined to get the word out at the annual Earth Day rally. Ladonna can't wait to experience her first blizzard, if only the weather would cooperate.

programme.title: An Arthur Thanksgiving
programme.desc: Arthur and his family are getting ready for Thanksgiving! But when Pal disappears to go on his own adventure, family, friends and the rest of Elwood City try to get Pal home. Will they be able to celebrate the best Thanksgiving yet?

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop forgets to say "please" and "thank you". Riff shares his dream of becoming an inventor.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: A Fountain of Fun
programme.desc: While Barney and the children wait for Mr. Boyd's new fountain to be delivered, they have fun playing in and exploring the park.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Play It Safe!
programme.desc: It's always fun to share a day with Barney—but it's also important to learn how to "play it safe!" Baby Bop, wanting to be a good helper, has been attaching adhesive strips to her frustrated (and uninjured) brother, BJ.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Day and Night
programme.desc: With a little Barney magic, the children experience the differences between day and night!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ wants to learn Terry's fancy basketball moves. After some advice from Barney, BJ has fun while doing his best! On a campout, Baby Bop sees a falling star and wants to return it to the sky.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Play for Exercise!
programme.desc: The children are surprised to learn that playing games and having fun in the park is great exercise. Baby Bop is happy to learn that not only is she exercising while she plays, but exercise will make her grow big and strong.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: The Music Box
programme.desc: The kids are having a yard sale, getting rid of old toys, books and clothes. Kioko shows up holding a prized possession—a music box from Switzerland. By accident, Ben includes the music box in a cardboard box of yard sale items!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: It's Showtime!
programme.desc: Barney, BJ and the kids spend the day preparing for a performance for their friends in the park.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Super Irma
programme.desc: While the Turtles are busy trying to decide what costumes to wear to this year's Channel Six Halloween party, April and Irma are doing a story on the reverse polarity magnet at Magno-Dyne Labs.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Adventures in Turtle-Sitting
programme.desc: While Splinter is away on meditative satori, Donatello sets his sights on solving world hunger. He invents the food revivafiver, a device designed to reverse the aging process of food.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Sword of Yurikawa
programme.desc: Krang plans to blow up the Tri-State Bridge just as a convoy of trucks carrying a rare form of toxic waste passes overhead.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Return of the Turtleoid
programme.desc: The Turtles go to the Top Notch Scientific Corporation to stand guard over the sonic disintegrator.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Shreeka's Revenge
programme.desc: Shreeka, an alien with an attitude and a vendetta against Krang, after having spent eons tracking him down, has finally found him.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Too Hot to Handle
programme.desc: Donatello tells the other three Turtles that, based on his calculations, the Earth has changed its orbit and is heading for the Sun.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Nightmare in the Lair
programme.desc: Down in the sewers, while Michelangelo, Leonardo, and Raphael are watching yet another cheesy sci-fi film, Donatello is busy building the dream-o-vision.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Phantom of the Sewers
programme.desc: After another night's performance of the robotic Willie Wombat Revue at the Willie Wombat Revue Theatre, a mysterious figure, wearing a long black cape, black fedora, white face mask, and known only as the Phantom, makes an appearance.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Leonardo is Missing
programme.desc: When Donatello, Michelangelo, and Raphael return from the video arcade they discover that Donatello's lab is in disarray and Leonardo is missing.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Snakes Alive!
programme.desc: While practicing their martial arts, Leonardo tells Splinter that he is convinced that there is one combat maneuver he has yet to teach the Turtles.
